input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate to the modern configuration format. Let me look at their project files to understand the current setup.

data.content:
The `.testsettings` format is **deprecated** in modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's what your settings map to:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property | `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code coverage DataCollector | `<DataCollectors>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |
| `AssemblyResolution` / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` (usually no longer needed) |

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Test timeout in milliseconds (was TestTimeout property in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Code coverage collector (was in AgentRule/DataCollectors) -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout in milliseconds -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Assembly resolution / deployment is handled automatically; disable if not needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment settings
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

With MSTest v3+, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The migration path is to use a `.runsettings` file instead.

Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file and explain the migration.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Test timeout in milliseconds (was TestTimeout property in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Code coverage collector (was in AgentRule/DataCollectors) -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout in milliseconds -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Assembly resolution / deployment is handled automatically; disable if not needed -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire it up in your `.csproj`:

Created `local.runsettings` and linked it in the `.csproj`. Key changes:

1. **Test timeout** → `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` under `<MSTest>` (per-test) and `<TestSessionTimeout>` (whole run).
2. **Code coverage** → moved to `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` with the v2.0 collector URI.
3. **Assembly resolution** → no longer needed; `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` disables legacy deployment. Set to `true` if your tests depend on deployed files.

You can now delete `local.testsettings`. Run tests with:

```
dotnet test
```

or explicitly:

```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```
